title: Star Wars: Force Commander
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|new|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Commander}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:SWFCbox.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Force Commander'' |developer=*[[LucasArts]] *[[Ronin Entertainment]] |publisher=LucasArts |release date=[[March 22]], [[2000]]{{WebCite|url=http://pc.ign.com/games/3732.html|text=Star Wars: Force Commander|archivedate=20000815221639}} |closed= |upc= |engine=Game Plus Plus Development Environment [[Ronin Entertainment]] |genre=Real Time Strategy |modes=Single player, multiplayer |ratings=[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]: Teen (T)
[[Wikipedia:Unterhaltungssoftware Selbstkontrolle|USK]]: 12 |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline= |prev= |next= }} '''''Star Wars: Force Commander''''' is a [[Wikipedia:Real-time strategy|real-time strategy]] (RTS) [[Star Wars video games|computer game]] released for the PC in [[March]] [[2000]]. ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=FORCE COMMANDER |text=It is a period of great strife. Feeling the weight of the Imperial fleet, countless worlds have fallen to the Galactic Empire. The Rebel Alliance has struck back, in space and on the ground, fortifying their positions and taking back systems in the name of freedom. Gathering in orbit around the placid world KALAAN, Imperial Star Destroyers prepare to attack a Rebel sanctuary and take that freedom away...}} ==Plot summary== {{Plot}} The game interweaves its story through the events of ''Episodes IV'' through ''VI'', sometimes intersecting with known Star Wars events, while telling an original Star Wars story. It is shown from the point of view of a young officer in the Imperial Navy, [[Brenn Tantor]], who begins as a stormtrooper, but soon enough is given his own command. The first task (from the training missions) is to search for an escape pod that landed on Tatooine, and then track the droids that were inside - which is a reference to the opening scenes of ''Episode IV'' when [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] escape Imperial clutches via an escape pod which crashes on Tatooine. The main character is loyal to the Empire for the first half of the game, until his brother, [[Dellis Tantor]] discovers that the Empire killed their father. Dellis is imprisoned by the Empire for revealing this information. Brenn defects to the Rebel Alliance and proceeds to fight his former commanders. As well as the [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] training missions, the player participates in the Battle of Hoth (from the Imperial point of view) and the Battle of Endor (as a Rebel). The game ends with the battle to capture the Imperial Palace on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and ultimately the liberation of Dellis. ==Gameplay== The game's action runs from immediately before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] through the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Coruscant]]. The player takes on the role of [[Brenn Tantor]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] commander who later defects to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Game features include over 40 different unit types, 24 campaign missions and 27 multiplayer missions, 12 3-D environments, and remixes of [[John Williams]]'s original ''Star Wars'' scores as music. Following a trend of its contemporary RTS titles, ''Force Commander'' eschewed the standard resource management system. Instead, the player gathers "Command Points" to grow his or her force. ===Missions=== *[[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|Desert Training]] *[[Assault on Ruul]] *[[Battle of Sarapin (Galactic Civil War)|Crisis on Sarapin]] *[[Rescue mission on Yavin 4|The Trap at Yavin IV]] *[[Battle of Hoth/Legends|The Battle of Hoth]] *[[Mission to Endor (Galactic Civil War)|Surprise at Endor]] *[[Massacre on Abridon|The Massacre on Abridon]] *[[Escape from Kalaan|Escape From Kalaan]] *[[Mission to Ruul (Galactic Civil War)|Return To Ruul]] *[[Mission to Trasse|The Walker Gambit]] *[[Liberation of Abridon|Redemption at Abridon]] *[[Battle of Endor/Legends|The Alliance Strikes Back]] *[[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|The Siege of Coruscant]] ==Development== Originally conceived as a 2-D strategy game, [[LucasArts]] scrapped the overall design after its first public viewing at the [[1998]] [[Wikipedia:Electronic Gaming Expo|Electronic Gaming Expo]]. Deciding to pursue a 3-D version, LucasArts licensed a game engine from [[Ronin Entertainment]] and recreated the game from scratch. The game was jointly developed by both companies, using Ronin's engine and programmers, partnered with LucasArts' project leadership, designers, and artists. ==Media==
